creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
(In reply to Nuno Teixeira from comment #5)
> Waiting on https://github.com/wxWidgets/wxWidgets/issues/24076

Why? Why are you waiting? The upstream is not responsible for FreeBSD ports, YOU are. It is not at all uncommon for ports to add patches for fixes not yet in the upstream. Indeed, that's one of the reasons we have patches to begin with!

Look under www/firefox/files, for example -- they have numerous patches there with bug-IDs referenced directly in the filenames!

Maybe, hesitation would've been justified, if the port worked without the fix -- if the patch was merely an improvement. But the port is flat out BROKEN for many users -- and the fix is a noop for those, for whom it currently works (people with both X11 and Wayland).

There really is no reason to wait.
